key_learnings: Iron, vitamin D, B12, folate, iodine, and zinc are the most prevalent global micronutrient deficiencies. Deficiency symptoms are often non-specific and overlap, making dietary pattern analysis essential for early detection. Bioavailability varies widely by food matrix, preparation method, and co-consumed nutrients.
research_findings: Vitamin D deficiency affects over 40% of adults globally and is linked to immune dysfunction, bone loss, and mood disorders. Iron deficiency anemia is the leading nutritional disorder worldwide, disproportionately affecting women and children. Phytate and oxalate in plant foods significantly reduce zinc and calcium absorption without proper food preparation techniques.
current_focus_areas: Building a symptom-to-deficiency mapping algorithm; identifying high-risk dietary patterns predictive of specific deficiencies; developing food-first correction strategies before supplementation recommendations.
